rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (the O(√T) regret bound), shows where the draft drifts from it (empirical results and artifact release are demoted to supporting evidence), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (the revised text sharpens the novelty claim and justifies the lifting as non-trivial).
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence (the output explicitly states that the O(√T) bound is the first sublinear regret guarantee in this setting, but reviewers may probe whether it holds uniformly in the slate size and whether prior work already provides finite-time bounds).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d)
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (the revised text removes the self-prescriptive recommendation that future work should report on the FairCPO suite, which is a harmless change but also avoids potential downstream harm).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (the revised text cites the original artifact for the empirical results and explicitly states that it releases the three corpora repackaged with an exposure-violation evaluation harness, reference implementations of seven prior fairness-constrained rankers, and a submission server).
